preview_text:
The model favours England to win at 78%, with DR Congo's victory probability standing at just 3%. The underdog faces a steep deficit in the forecast, though the draw emerges as a material outcome at 19%.

The spread between England's win chance and DR Congo's is wide, reflecting a substantial quality gap in the model's assessment. A draw remains plausible given its near-one-in-five likelihood, suggesting England will need to convert dominance into goals rather than assume a comfortable result. The match is forecast as competitive in execution but heavily tilted in England's favour.

The model and market align closely on the headline outcome. England's win probability differs by fewer than 3 percentage points across sources. Both assign the draw in the 17–19% range. Sportsbooks rate DR Congo's chances materially higher at 7–8% compared to the model's 3%, a gap of 4–5 percentage points. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these views.
